item.title: Blackstone’s $343 Million Loan on Chicago Skyscraper Defaults
item.description: A Blackstone Inc. loan on an office tower in the heart of Chicago’s financial center has gone into default, the latest sign of the persistent challenges landlords face after the city’s commercial real estate values tumbled in the wake of the pandemic.

item.title: Estados Unidos, China y Europa aceleran la carrera por el liderazgo cuántico
item.description: Ya llegó la computación cuántica. Pero, ¿qué es?

item.title: Sunrun Surges Over 30% on Tesla Deal to Supply Power for AI
item.description: Sunrun Inc.’s stock jumped as much as 31% after the home solar provider announced it will partner with Tesla Inc. and Renew Home to provide power for data centers and utilities across the US.

item.title: ECB’s Schnabel Sees Need for Further Hiking to Meet 2% Target
item.description: The European Central Bank will probably have to increase borrowing costs further to tame price pressures, Executive Board member Isabel Schnabel told Die Zeit newspaper.

item.title: Hyperscalers Ditch Canada Investor Calls and Buyers Don’t Care
item.description: Two of the world’s largest technology companies swiftly raised C$22.5 billion ($15.8 billion) in the Canadian debt market to support their spending plans on artificial intelligence. Investors are now eagerly awaiting another one.

item.title: Seth Klarman, Man Group Spot Bargains After Brazil Bond Wipeouts
item.description: Famed value investor Seth Klarman offered a surprising answer at a conference this month when asked where he’s seeing opportunities in the world of distressed assets.

item.title: Milei Shuns Wall Street to Raise Dollars at Rate Lower Than 7%
item.description: Argentine Economy Minister Luis Caputo’s strategy of resisting calls to raise funds in international markets appears to have paid off.

item.title: US New-Home Sales Unexpectedly Fall Amid High Mortgage Rates
item.description: Sales of new homes in the US fell again in May to the lowest level since the start of the year as heavy discounts failed to offset high mortgage rates.

item.title: Swiss Open Talks on Air Defense System to Diversify From Patriot
item.description: Switzerland is opening contract talks for a new long-range air defense system, while scaling back a planned sales-tax increase intended to help fund its military buildup.

item.title: JPMorgan Lifts S&P 500 Target to 7,800 as ‘Blue Sky’ Scenario Approaches
item.description: JPMorgan Chase & Co. believes US stocks are approaching their “blue sky” scenario, highlighting stronger-than-expected earnings growth and a potential peace deal to end the Iran war as key drivers.

item.title: Key Oil Spread Flips to Contango as Supply From Hormuz Climbs
item.description: A closely-watched oil market indicator flipped into a bearish structure for the first time since February, as the US-Iran agreement to reopen the Strait of Hormuz boosts flows from the Middle East.

item.title: Chile Central Bank Vows Caution in Policy as Price Risks Decline
item.description: Chile’s central bank said it will remain cautious in the conduct of monetary policy in the face of good news from the interim US-Iran peace deal, according to the minutes to its last policy meeting.

item.title: Wendy's Surges 37% Following Viral Reddit Post
item.description: The meme-stock crowd is rallying behind another beaten up American icon — Wendy’s Co., the fast-food chain known for the Frosty, Dave’s Triple burger and the Biggie Deal.

item.title: Google’s New $100 Speaker Makes a Weak Case for Gemini in the Home
item.description: The Google Home Speaker is the first new device of its kind to come from Alphabet Inc. in six years. In that time, Google has reshaped itself as an industry leader in artificial intelligence, with its Gemini platform now ubiquitous across consumer hardware like smartphones and web services like Search, Workspace and YouTube.

item.title: OpenAI, Broadcom Unveil Chip to Run Models Faster, Cheaper
item.description: OpenAI unveiled its first custom artificial intelligence chip developed in partnership with Broadcom Inc., part of a bid by the ChatGPT maker to gain an edge by tailoring hardware to better run its AI products.

item.title: SK Hynix $29 Billion US Listing to Seize on Memory Frenzy
item.description: SK Hynix Inc. is seeking 45.45 trillion won ($29.4 billion) in a US listing, tapping investor demand for high-flying memory-chip stocks even after a major selloff shook the group this week.

item.title: Amazon Seller Reveals Rare Glimpse of Shadow Bribery Market
item.description: Middlemen lurking on message apps like WeChat offer access to company employees they say can grant favors for a price.

item.title: Hertz Slides After Profit Warning on Used Car ‘Softness’
item.description: Hertz Global Holdings Inc. shares tumbled the most in two years after the car renter warned profit this quarter is trending toward the low end of its expectations and announced offerings of shares and notes.

item.title: Ramaphosa Taps Former Treasury Budget Chief as Economic Adviser
item.description: South African President Cyril Ramaphosa appointed former National Treasury budget chief Michael Sachs as his economic adviser, bringing a respected fiscal policy technocrat back into the government as the country seeks to accelerate growth and deepen economic reforms.

item.title: Microsoft Says Copilot AI Helped Knock Down Cybercrime Tools
item.description: Investigators used new tools to defeat old malware technology.

item.title: Bitcoin’s Retreating Retail Army Exposes Fresh Market Fragility
item.description: Bitcoin spent years trying to shed its reputation as a playground for speculative retail traders. Its latest selloff is exposing the tradeoff: Wall Street brought scale and legitimacy, but the retail buyers who once helped absorb sharp declines have largely vanished.

item.title: Mexico’s Annual Inflation Eases More Than Expected Ahead of Rate Decision
item.description: Mexico’s annual inflation eased far more than expected in early June, according to a report that comes one day before the central bank is expected to hold its interest rate steady at the lowest level since 2022.

item.title: Bessent Says Iran Talks Feature Shift to Dollar Invoicing
item.description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent indicated that US talks with Iran include the longtime American adversary joining the dollar system and invoicing its oil sales in the greenback.

item.title: Qualcomm to Buy Modular For $3.9 Billion to Help AI Push
item.description: Qualcomm Inc. said it is acquiring Modular Inc., for about $3.9 billion in stock, adding artificial intelligence software capabilities that will aid its push into data centers.

item.title: Biotech Visionary Is Skeptical About AI’s Impact on Medical Innovation
item.description: The inventor of the groundbreaking Crispr gene-editing technology is skeptical about artificial intelligence replacing human effort in the realm of scientific discovery, adding to a growing debate over its impact on medical research and innovation.

item.title: Latin America’s Two Oil Giants Team Up in Search for Their Next Boost
item.description: Petrobras and Pemex have agreed to work together to discover, produce and refine oil.

item.title: Canada’s AtkinsRealis Seeks US Approval for Nuclear Tech to Power AI Boom
item.description: The surge in new data centers for artificial intelligence is translating into growth for the US electricity sector, and Canadian nuclear company AtkinsRealis Group Inc. wants in.

item.title: ‘Essex Boys’ Oil Traders Make Charity Offer to End UK Regulatory Probe
item.description: A group of British commodity day traders have offered to donate £1 million ($1.3 million) to charity to close a UK competition probe that looked at whether the sharing of potentially sensitive information distorted markets.

item.title: Dubai Holding Weighs Stake in Bain’s Data Center Group Hscale
item.description: Dubai Holding is exploring an investment in private equity-backed data center builder Hscale, people familiar with the matter said, as it seeks to grow its European portfolio.

item.title: Lingyi iTech Raises $1.1 Billion With Shares Priced at Maximum
item.description: China’s Lingyi iTech Guangdong Co. raised HK$8.3 billion ($1.1 billion) after pricing its Hong Kong listing at the maximum price, with strong investor demand leading the company to reject more than 100 orders, according to people familiar with the matter.
